body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
`ᶠnh_pressure₃ʲs` is used in both `edmfx_nh_pressure_tendency!` and `edmfx_tke_tendency!`. Before it was calculated in `edmfx_nh_pressure_tendency!`, so we have to call it before `edmfx_tke_tendency!`. This PR moves it to `set_prognostic_edmf_precomputed_quantities_closures!` so the order of tendencies doesn't matter.

Closes #3735 

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

title: move nh pressure to edmf precomputed quantities

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
We are currently using `zero_velocity_tendency!` in the EDMFX (prospct) advection test's tendency, but we are not making any corresponding update to the Jacobian. This PR adds a `zero_velocity_jacobian!` function that zeros out the corresponding Jacobian blocks.

title: Fix Jacobian for zero_velocity_tendency

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
We have recently been seeing timeout errors for the following jobs: CI on Ubuntu with Julia 1.11, CI on Windows with Julia 1.11, and Lookahead on Ubuntu with the nightly release of Julia. This PR bumps the timeout limit for CI with Julia 1.11 to 60 minutes (while keeping the timeout for 1.10 at 45 minutes), and it switches the OS for the Lookahead job from Ubuntu to MacOS (we only need to test one OS for this job, so we can just use the one with the shortest compilation times).
